claim: Marble is the first multimodal frontier world model; it can generate 3D worlds from text, images, video, or coarse 3D layouts; supports user navigation and editing
actionable: Use Marble for rapid 3D environment prototyping from images or text; test world generation for video game levels, architectural visualization, or training simulations

claim: World models enable infinite-scale agent training: recreate real environments digitally, train embodied agents (robots) in simulation, avoid costly real-world data collection
actionable: For robotics/embodied AI, generate training worlds with Marble instead of collecting real-world data; train agents in simulation before real deployment

claim: Marble can turn single 2D images into navigable 3D worlds; it can also merge multiple images and perform consistent edits (e.g., change wall color, swap objects)
actionable: Use Marble for interior design visualization: photograph your space, generate 3D, then edit to preview renovations with realistic rendering

claim: Generated 3D worlds degrade at the edges (blurriness) as you move further; the vision is infinite-scale generation as you navigate (not yet implemented)
actionable: When using Marble, be aware of limited generation depth; future versions should support infinite generation as you explore
